source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the ER- 3, ER-2, and ER-1 Zones Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the ER- 3, ER-2, and ER-1 Zones Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the ER- 3, ER-2, and ER-1 Zones Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within an ER- 3, ER-2, or ER-1 zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed
provisions.text: Subject to Section 103 and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 228(2), any portion of a main building,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 228(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 228(2), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 228(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 3.0 metres. Side Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: in an ER-3 or ER-2 zone, a townhouse dwelling use shall have a minimum required side setback of: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: 0.0 metre along a common wall between each unit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: 3.0 metres elsewhere;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: in an ER-3 or ER-2 zone, a semi-detached dwelling use or duplex apartment use shall have a minimum required side setback of 0.0 metre along a common wall between each unit; or (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: 1.25 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: The minimum side setback requirements for Established Residential Special Areas, as shown on Schedule 3C, are as specified in Table 9 as follows: Table 9: Minimum required side setbacks for Established Residential Special Areas Established Residential Special Area Minimum Required Side Setback Grant Street (GS) 1.5 metres Young Avenue (YA) 10% of the lot width, to a maximum of 3.0 metres Oakland Road (OR) 10% of the lot width, to a maximum of 2.0 metres Armview (AV) 4.5 metres North End Halifax 1 (NEH-1) 1.5 metres on one side, 0.0 metre on the other North End Halifax 2 (NEH-2) 0.0 metre Dartmouth North 1 (DN-1) 2.0 metres Dartmouth North 2 (DN-2) 2.5 metres Historic Dartmouth Neighbourhoods (HDN) 2.0 metres on one side, 0.0 metre on the other

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 229(2) and Section 234, the minimum
provisions.text: There is no maximum required side setback. Rear Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: The minimum required rear setback shall be 6.0 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: The minimum required rear setback shall be 6.0 metres.
provisions.text: The minimum required rear setback shall be 6.0 metres. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the maximum required lot coverage shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: for a single-unit dwelling use, 40%; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: for all other uses,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: if a lot is larger than 325.0 square metres, 50%, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: if a lot is 325.0 square metres or smaller, 60%. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 231(3), the maximum required lot coverage for Established Residential Special Areas, as shown on Schedule 3C, are listed in Table 10 as follows: Table 10: Maximum required lot coverage for Established Residential Special Areas Established Residential Special Area Maximum Required Lot Coverage (%) Grant Street (GS) 35% Young Avenue (YA) 35% North End Halifax 2 (NEH-2) 50% (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 231(2) and 231(3), and Section 234, the
provisions.text: One accessory structure per lot, which has a footprint that is no greater than 20.0 square metres, shall be exempted from the maximum required lot coverage calculation. Residential Density by Lot Area

sections.title_label_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more than

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: 4 dwelling units if a lot is less than 375.0 square metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: 5 dwelling units if a lot is at least 375.0 square metres but less than 450.0 square metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: 6 dwelling units if a lot is at least 450.0 square metres but less than 525.0 square metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: 7 dwelling units if a lot is at least 525.0 square metres but less than 600.0 square metres; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: In an ER-3 zone, a multi-unit dwelling use shall contain no more
provisions.text: 8 dwelling units if a lot is at least 600.0 square metres. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Pedestrian Entrance on a Streetline

sections.title_label_raw: A multi-unit dwelling use shall have a required minimum of one

provisions.heading_context_raw: A multi-unit dwelling use shall have a required minimum of one
provisions.text: A multi-unit dwelling use shall have a required minimum of one exterior pedestrian entrance on a streetwall. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) Attached Garages

sections.title_label_raw: For any garage which is attached to a low-density dwelling

provisions.heading_context_raw: For any garage which is attached to a low-density dwelling
provisions.text: For any garage which is attached to a low-density dwelling use, the garage door(s) facing the front or flanking lot line shall not project any closer to the streetline than the longest wall of the attached main dwelling that faces the same streetline as the garage door(s).

provisions.heading_context_raw: For any garage which is attached to a low-density dwelling
provisions.text: For any garage which is attached to a low-density dwelling use, garage doors facing the front or flanking lot line shall not exceed a maximum required width of 50% of the wall of the attached main dwelling, to a maximum of 6.0 metres in total. Maximum Building Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the average finished grade, a low-density dwelling use, or any public building use, any main building shall not exceed: (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Sep 20/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: except as provided in Subsection 233(2) or 233(3), a building width of 20.0 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: a building depth of 30.0 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: The maximum building width of a townhouse block is 64.0 metres and the maximum number of permitted townhouse units in a townhouse block located in a ER-3 Zone is eight.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: An addition to an existing main building shall only be permitted in the rear yard but shall not exceed the building width or footprint of the existing main building, if the addition causes the main building to contain

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: more than 2 dwelling units in an ER-2 zone; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metres above the
provisions.text: more than 8 dwelling units in an ER-3 zone. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Additional Built Form Requirements within the Young Avenue Sub- Area A (YA-A)

sections.title_label_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit dwelling use, or a multi-unit dwelling use that contains up to 5 units within the Young Avenue Sub-Area A (YA-A), as shown on Schedule 3C, the following additional built form requirements shall apply: (RC- (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: a maximum lot coverage of 35%;

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: a minimum side setback of 10% of the lot width, to a maximum of 3.0 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: For a two-unit dwelling use, a three-unit dwelling use, a four-unit
provisions.text: no less than 70% of the area of the roof must have a minimum pitch of 2/12 rise to run. Accessory Structures

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter
provisions.text: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
